cta.heading: Need a plant-specific recommendation?
cta.body: Share MW capacity, tilt or tracker layout, and soiling profile. Taypro engineers will map GLYDE, NYUMA, HELYX, or Taypro Opex to your asset.

meta.title: Taypro vs Indian Solar Cleaning Robot Companies — Full Comparison

hero.title: How Taypro compares to other solar panel cleaning robot companies in India
hero.lead: India's solar panel cleaning robot market is growing fast, with dozens of manufacturers competing for utility-scale O&M contracts. This page compares Taypro against Solabot Technologies, Aegeus Technologies, Skilancer Solar, and Vayu Solar on the criteria that matter to plant operators and EPC teams.

indianCompetitors.intro: All data below is sourced from each company's official website. Where a competitor does not publish a figure, it is marked as not disclosed.
indianCompetitors.sourceNote: Figures reflect publicly available information as of June 2026. Verify current specs on your RFQ before procurement decisions.
indianCompetitors.tableHeading: At a glance: Taypro vs competitors

0.taypro: Patented dual-pass: airflow first pass + self-cleaning microfiber drum

1.taypro: 4 Indian patents on dual-pass cleaning system and RF mesh communications

4.taypro: Yes — GLYDE-X (dual-pass) and NYUMA-X (PBT), purpose-built for single-axis trackers

7.taypro: NECTYR — schedules, block-wise cleaning logs, SCADA, AMC tickets, export reports

8.taypro: LTE, Wi-Fi, hybrid self-healing RF mesh, LoRa, LoRaWAN

9.taypro: Yes — Taypro OPEX: pay-per-panel-cleaned, Taypro operates the fleet
9.aegeus: Yes — MCaaS (Machine Cleaning as a Service)

11.taypro: Not published as a single fleet number (272 robots at Agar 200 MW alone)

14.taypro: LONGi, Solex, Premier Energies, RenewSys, Satvik, Trina Solar

15.taypro: Yes — pan-India, NECTYR remote diagnostics + field engineers

17.taypro: ORION plant intelligence platform (early access), CRADYL autonomous row-transfer docking

18.taypro: Not in top 5 by shipment volume (2025 Mercom leaderboard)

0.title: The only patented dual-pass cleaning system in India
0.body: Taypro holds 4 Indian patents covering the dual-pass mechanism — airflow first pass to dislodge loose particulate without surface contact, followed by a rotating self-cleaning microfiber drum for a complete wipe. The self-cleaning drum is a practical requirement at utility scale: a robot cleaning 3,600 modules per charge cannot deposit accumulated dust back onto cleaned panels. No other Indian manufacturer publishes an equivalent patented dual-pass system.

1.title: The only company with dedicated tracker robots across two cleaning technologies
1.body: Taypro offers tracker-compatible robots in both cleaning technologies — <glydeX>GLYDE-X</glydeX> for dual-pass microfiber and <nyumaX>NYUMA-X</nyumaX> for PBT — both purpose-engineered for single-axis tracker plants with flexible bodies and 360° bridge articulation. Solabot offers the SB-04T for trackers. Aegeus, Skilancer, and Vayu do not confirm tracker support on their websites.

2.title: The most comprehensive fleet software
2.body: NECTYR supports LTE, Wi-Fi, hybrid self-healing RF mesh, LoRa, and LoRaWAN — five connectivity options sized per site by Taypro engineers during commissioning. Competitors offer single-protocol solutions: Aegeus uses LoRaWAN, Solabot uses SCADA (protocol unspecified). NECTYR also includes block-wise scheduling, cleaning logs, AMC ticketing, and investor-grade report exports — features not published by any competitor.

3.title: TÜV NORD hardware certification
3.body: Taypro is the only Indian solar cleaning robot manufacturer with TÜV NORD certified hardware, validated for damp-heat and dry-heat conditions. Aegeus holds UL India accreditation for its Unicorn robot. No certification is published by Solabot, Skilancer, or Vayu.

4.title: Named module OEM approvals
4.body: Taypro publicly names its approved module manufacturers: LONGi, Solex, Premier Energies, RenewSys, Satvik, and Trina Solar. Solabot claims 16 Tier 1 approvals but does not name them. Other competitors make general claims. Named approvals matter for warranty compliance on large IPP portfolios.

5.title: Largest single-deployment scale on record
5.body: Taypro's Agar 200 MW deployment of 272 robots is the largest publicly documented single-plant robotic cleaning fleet in India with verified data: 265 GLYDE automatic units, 7 semi-automatic, commissioned in 2024, generating an additional 7.50 GWh/year and saving 28 million litres of water annually. No competitor publishes equivalent plant-level data with robot counts and measured outcomes.

6.title: OPEX managed service with named commercial structure
6.body: Taypro Opex is a pay-per-panel-cleaned managed service where Taypro operates the fleet on the plant — with pricing transparency, defined cleaning cycles (3–10 per month based on soiling study), and full NECTYR reporting. Aegeus offers MCaaS as a service model. Solabot, Skilancer, and Vayu do not clearly publish an equivalent managed service on their websites.

competitorAdvantages.heading: Where competitors have advantages
competitorAdvantages.intro: An honest comparison requires acknowledging where other companies lead on specific metrics.

0.title: Solabot leads on robot count
0.body: — 12,422+ robots installed across 152+ projects is a larger installed fleet by units than Taypro's published numbers. Solabot also claims compatibility certificates from 16 Tier 1 module manufacturers, which is a broader OEM approval footprint. Their Mercom 2025 #2 ranking reflects this volume-based strength.

1.title: Aegeus leads on rooftop
1.body: — with Shreem specifically designed for rooftop installations and a longer track record in that segment. Taypro's MINY rooftop robot is on the roadmap but not yet shipping.

2.title: Skilancer leads on project count
2.body: — 300+ projects (though no GW or robot count is published), with named clients including Renew Power 105 MW and AvaadaClean 100 MW.

howToRead.heading: How to read this comparison
howToRead.body: Robot count and project count measure market penetration. GW protected and cleaning technology depth measure operational capability at scale. For a 50–250 MW utility plant evaluating a robot partner, the questions that matter are: does the cleaning technology protect your module warranty, does the fleet software give you audit-ready O&M data, can the partner support same-day breakdown across India, and is there a commercial model that matches your balance sheet? On each of those criteria, Taypro publishes specific, verifiable answers — which is why this comparison draws only from official website sources.
howToRead.ctaLine: To evaluate Taypro's robots for your plant, <contact>request a quote</contact> or run a directional payback estimate on the <calculator>ROI calculator</calculator>. To compare individual robot models, see the <hub>full product hub</hub>.

0.q: Why isn't Taypro in the Mercom 2025 top 5?
0.a: Mercom ranks by 2025 shipment volume. Taypro's strength is documented in GW deployed, plant-level case studies, and technology depth — not unit shipment leaderboard position. Evaluate vendors on your plant's criteria, not ranking methodology alone.

1.q: Which Indian competitor has the most robots installed?
1.a: Solabot publishes 12,422+ robots installed on its homepage — the largest disclosed fleet count among Indian competitors. Taypro documents 5 GW+ deployed capacity and 272 robots at a single 200 MW plant.

2.q: Should we run a head-to-head trial?
2.a: Yes. Shortlist two vendors, run a 30–60 day soiling trial on representative blocks, and compare PR uplift, cycle time, logged coverage, and breakdown response before final procurement.

meta.title: Solar Panel Cleaning Robot vs Manual Cleaning in India
meta.description: Compare robotic vs manual solar panel cleaning for Indian utility plants: water use, labour safety, cycle frequency, fleet data, and when each model fits.

hero.title: Solar panel cleaning robot vs manual cleaning
hero.lead: Manual wet washing and robotic waterless cleaning both remove soiling, but they differ on water availability, labour risk, proof of completion, and long-run cost on MW-scale plants.

robotVsManual.intro: Utility IPPs in Rajasthan, Gujarat, and Madhya Pradesh often start with manual crews or tractor-mounted brushes, then evaluate robots when soiling losses, water cost, or safety incidents rise. This page compares decision criteria—not a single winner for every site.

0.manual: Typically requires water; scarce on arid sites

1.robot: Fewer rooftop/row walks; scheduled autonomous cycles
1.manual: Higher crew hours; heat and height exposure

2.manual: Often photo/checklist based; harder to audit at scale

3.robot: Higher frequency feasible without proportional labour
3.manual: Cost and crew limits reduce cadence

4.robot: CAPEX robots or Taypro Opex per-panel service

5.manual: Small C&I, abundant water, very low soiling

0.q: When does a robot beat manual cleaning?
0.a: When soiling losses exceed labour+water cost, water is limited, or owners need logged cleaning coverage for lenders and O&M contracts. Run Taypro's ROI calculator with your PR data.

1.q: Can robots and manual cleaning coexist?
1.a: Yes. Many plants use robots on bulk arrays and manual methods on switchyards, substations, or damaged tables until robots are deployed fleet-wide.

0.title: Patented dual-pass cleaning technology

1.title: NECTYR multi-protocol fleet software
1.body: NECTYR offers five connectivity options (LTE, Wi-Fi, RF mesh, LoRa, LoRaWAN) with block-wise logs, AMC ticketing, and investor-grade exports. Solabot's SB-SCADA does not publish equivalent feature depth.

2.title: TÜV NORD hardware certification and named OEM approvals
2.body: Taypro holds TÜV NORD certification and names six approved module manufacturers. Solabot claims 16 Tier 1 approvals but does not publish manufacturer names — a gap for warranty compliance audits.

3.title: OPEX managed service and largest single-plant deployment
3.body: Taypro Opex offers pay-per-panel-cleaned managed cleaning with NECTYR reporting. Taypro's Agar 200 MW deployment (272 robots, +7.50 GWh/year verified) is the largest documented single-plant fleet with measured outcomes.

competitorStrengths.heading: Where Solabot leads
competitorStrengths.body: Solabot leads on installed robot count — 12,422+ units across 152+ projects is the largest published fleet number among Indian competitors. Solabot also ships a rooftop robot (SB-RT) today while Taypro's MINY is on the roadmap, and holds Mercom 2025 #2 ranking by shipment volume. Solabot claims 16 Tier 1 module compatibility certificates — a broader OEM footprint if verified on your RFQ.

0.q: Should we run a head-to-head trial?
0.a: Yes—shortlist two vendors, run a 30–60 day soiling trial on representative blocks, and compare PR uplift, cycle time, and logged coverage.

1.q: Does Taypro replace Solabot on existing plants?
1.a: Taypro deploys on greenfield and retrofit utility sites. Migration depends on dock compatibility and O&M contract terms—scope in technical due diligence.

2.q: Why does Solabot publish more robots than Taypro?
2.a: Solabot counts total units shipped; Taypro publishes GW deployed and plant-level case studies. Compare on your plant's technology fit, software audit needs, and service SLA—not unit totals alone.

2.q: Why compare Taypro and Vayu if Taypro publishes more data?

meta.title: Waterless vs Water-Based Solar Panel Cleaning
meta.description: Compare waterless robotic and water-based manual solar cleaning: dust removal, module risk, water cost, environmental compliance, and Indian utility fit.

hero.title: Waterless vs water-based solar panel cleaning
hero.lead: Water washing remains common on smaller sites; waterless robots dominate new Indian utility RFQs where water is scarce and audit cadence is high.

waterlessVsWater.intro: Dust chemistry, module coating, and local water cost determine the right method. Taypro standardises on waterless dual-pass and PBT dry cleaning for utility arrays; water-based methods may still suit specific zones with reliable supply and low TDS.

0.waterless: None on-row; major savings in arid states
0.water: Litres per wash × table count × cycles

3.waterless: No runoff; aligns with water-scarce site permits

5.water: Low soiling C&I with cheap water access

0.a: Taypro uses dry microfiber and PBT methods designed for utility glass—validate on a pilot block with your module OEM guidance.

1.q: Can we switch from wet to waterless mid-life?
1.a: Yes. Retrofit robots on live plants; Taypro Opex can bridge until CAPEX approval.
